name: Product Roadmap — Q3 Prioritization
description: A product team with 3 squads deciding what to build next quarter. 15 feature requests, limited capacity. The projector reveals which features are structural pillars (build these first) vs isolated wishes.
direction: Maximize customer retention and expansion revenue with 3 squads over one quarter

tensions.claim: SSO is table stakes for enterprise deals but only 12% of current customers need it — building for future pipeline not current users
Mobile usage is growing 15% month over month but the mobile experience requires rethinking the entire interaction model
API v1 has 40 integration partners but the architecture limits what partners can build — v2 unlocks new use cases but breaks existing integrations
Time to value is 14 days — halving it doubles activation rate but requires simplifying features customers eventually need
Customers ask for analytics but what they really want is proof of ROI to justify renewal to their CFO
Automation reduces manual work by 60% for power users but increases complexity for new users who haven't learned the basics
Multi-tenant architecture enables agency use case which is 30% of pipeline but changes the permission model fundamentally
Dashboard loads in 4 seconds — acceptable but competitors load in 1 second and perception of speed equals perception of quality
AI suggestions increase engagement 2x in pilot but model accuracy at 78% means 1 in 5 suggestions is wrong and erodes trust
Current billing can't handle usage-based pricing which is what enterprise wants but billing migration is invisible to users and high risk
Real-time collaboration is the top requested feature but multiplayer architecture is a 6-month rewrite of the core data layer
French and German markets represent 25% of pipeline but localization isn't just translation — it's date formats, compliance, payment methods
Self-serve upgrade path would reduce sales team dependency but current users expect white-glove onboarding for premium tiers
